name: Chicken Roll-Ups
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces can crescent dinner rolls, (see note)
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate dough into 8 triangles and flatten each slightly.
2. Place about 2 teaspoons of cheese, 1 teaspoon of barbecue sauce, and 1 chicken chunk on the shortest side of each triangle.
3. Fold two corners over the chicken chunk; roll to the opposite point.
4. Pinch edges together to seal.
5. Place the point side down on a greased cookie sheet.
6. Brush with egg; sprinkle with sesame seeds.
7. Bake in a preheated 375 degree F oven for 12 to 18 minutes, or until golden brown. Makes 8 roll-ups.
NOTE: Tonight I used one of the recipes for chicken pocket dough (I used one less teaspoon of sugar in mine). I liked it much better. The rollups didn't seem as rich as with the crescent rolls. I put double the recipe in each pocket, though otherwise there seemed to be too much dough. I also left off the sesame seeds. The kids think they don't like them.
